authoritative_sources.title: Hotel, Hospitality, and Tourism Glossary
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CoStar STR is a recognized hotel benchmarking and performance data provider. Its public glossary is authoritative for hotel metric terminology such as occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, TrevPAR, RGI, comp set, supply, demand, inventory, and segmentation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Hospitality metric definition and source-specification intake
used_for: occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, TrevPAR, and RGI terminology
rooms-available, rooms-sold, and segmentation denominator control
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains hospitality KPI definitions so the lane records denominator, inventory, segmentation, comp-set, and comparability assumptions before producing analysis.

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection authority for unfair or deceptive commerce practices. Its fee guidance is authoritative for short-term lodging price, fee, total-price, refundability, and advertising constraints that affect analytics claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fee, refundability, and guest-facing disclosure review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents analytics outputs from treating fee, refundability, or guest-facing pricing conclusions as legally approved disclosure.

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ Title III ADA regulations are authoritative for public accommodations and places of lodging, including accessible-room reservation handling, equal-access obligations, and guest-impact boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accessible-room inventory, booking, and service-failure analytics
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces accessible-room analysis to preserve PMS, CRS, room-block, and reservation evidence while routing legal compliance conclusions to accessibility or legal owners.

authoritative_sources.title: Technical Directive Reporting On-Time Performance 2026
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary travel operating and reporting guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BTS is the DOT statistical agency for transportation data. Its technical directive is authoritative for airline on-time performance source requirements, reportable carriers, reporting cadence, CRS/OAG schedule references, and flight-performance reporting boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Travel performance source validation and disclosure review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains travel performance analytics so the lane records data source, carrier, schedule, and reporting-scope assumptions rather than using generic travel metrics.

authoritative_sources.title: Commission Guidance on Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI guidance is authoritative for how operational metrics must be defined, contextualized, caveated, and updated when they are used in MD&A, investor-style reporting, or formal disclosures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Executive, board, investor-style, and public disclosure review
used_for: unsupported benchmark and public claim refusal
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires explicit metric definitions, assumptions, source lineage, and update posture before the lane drafts board, website, or investor-style language.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Measures Inventory Tool
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS quality-measure inventory is authoritative for CMS measure identification, stewarding, measure programs, and measure specification references used to validate numerator, denominator, exclusion, and reporting context.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Healthcare quality metric definition and source-specification intake
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains healthcare-adjacent hospitality analytics that reference quality, access, utilization, benchmark, or public-reporting measures.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Measures Management System Blueprint
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS measure-management guidance is authoritative for measure lifecycle, specification, testing, implementation, maintenance, and evidence expectations used to constrain healthcare metric claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Measure lifecycle, specification, and maintenance control
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents tenant dashboard labels from replacing formal measure logic when hospitality analysis touches quality-measure or patient-access reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official HHS HIPAA guidance is authoritative for limiting protected health information in analytics outputs, examples, disclosure packets, and escalations to the minimum necessary for the stated purpose.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling, audience review, and disclosure minimization
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Blocks broad sharing of patient/member examples, low-volume cohorts, or identifiable healthcare lodging analysis without purpose, audience, consent, and approval context.

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS claims-processing manuals are authoritative for Medicare claim workflow, provider billing record context, claim-state semantics, and payer/provider operational evidence used in encounter-to-claim reconciliation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Encounter, charge, claim, remittance, and denial validation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents folio, encounter, charge, claim, remittance, and denial records from being treated as interchangeable when healthcare lodging or travel reimbursement metrics are requested.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS prior-authorization guidance is authoritative for prior-authorization process, documentation, payer response, and patient-access workflow dependencies that can gate analytics interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Eligibility, referral, authorization, and payer-dependency checks
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires explicit authorization, referral, eligibility, service-date, and payer/plan facts before analyzing patient lodging, delayed access, utilization, or denial metrics.

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official ONC interoperability baseline is authoritative for healthcare data classes and terminology used to trace patient, encounter, problem, procedure, medication, laboratory, and clinical-operational evidence in analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Healthcare data lineage and terminology normalization
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps healthcare-adjacent hospitality analytics grounded in recognized patient, encounter, and clinical-operational data classes without converting them into clinical interpretation.

authoritative_sources.title: eCQI Resource Center
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / eCQI Resource Center
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal eCQI resource is authoritative for eCQM specifications, QRDA implementation guidance, value sets, and standards-based quality-measure evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains healthcare-adjacent hospitality analytics that mention eCQM, QRDA, value-set, or standards-based quality evidence so the lane preserves measure artifacts and avoids improvised quality reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: HEDIS Measures and Technical Resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCQA is the steward of HEDIS; its public measure and technical-resource guidance is authoritative for health-plan quality measure terminology, denominator, exclusion, and reporting workflow expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Health-plan quality measure and benchmark handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains plan-quality or access claims tied to hospitality support workflows so the lane records denominator, exclusion, benchmark, and comparability limits.

workflow_stages.name: Tenant, audience, property, market, patient/member, and reporting-context intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, business unit, property, brand, market, program, product, payer, plan, facility, provider type, service setting, reporting period, intended audience, disclosure posture, accessibility context, privacy posture, and source-precedence rules before analysis starts.
source_classes: Primary hospitality, travel, or consumer-protection regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and dependency validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate room inventory, room status, accessible-room availability, booking channel, group block, itinerary, eligibility, referral, authorization, service date, coverage, consent, and documentation dependencies when they affect hospitality, travel, access, utilization, claim, denial, or operational metrics.
source_classes: Primary hospitality, travel, or consumer-protection regulator guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and measure logic control
workflow_stages.description: Define numerator, denominator, exclusions, attribution, cohort, room-inventory treatment, channel treatment, comp-set posture, refresh window, source tables, measure references, thresholds, cadence, and publication limits before calculation or narrative synthesis.
source_classes: Recognized hospitality, travel, healthcare workflow, or measure guidance
Public metric-definition, reporting, disclosure, or benchmark guidance

workflow_stages.name: Source collection, reconciliation, and confidence scoring
workflow_stages.description: Collect and reconcile PMS, CRS, RMS, channel manager, POS, CRM, loyalty, event, travel, payment, incident, EHR, EDW, referral, authorization, charge, claim, remittance, denial, quality, utilization-review, and BI records while preserving source conflicts and lag assumptions.
source_classes: Recognized hospitality, travel, healthcare workflow, or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Analysis package assembly and caveat drafting
workflow_stages.description: Assemble metric specifications, analytical summaries, caveats, confidence limits, threshold notes, source-lineage notes, exception registers, and recommended next questions for the approved audience and cadence.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, disclosure, or benchmark guidance

workflow_stages.name: Privacy, disclosure, and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop when PHI handling, guest or traveler privacy, payment-card sensitivity, minimum-necessary posture, public reporting, fee disclosure, accessibility compliance, clinical judgment, patient safety, payer interpretation, coding policy, legal interpretation, quality attestation, pricing approval, refund approval, fraud review, or operational approval is required.
source_classes: Primary hospitality, travel, or consumer-protection regulator guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

artifacts: tenant policy, jurisdiction, business unit, property, brand, market, program, product, payer, plan, provider type, facility type, service setting, and reporting-audience records

PMS room inventory, room status, occupancy, reservation, CRS, RMS, channel manager, group block, event, rate, promotion, folio, POS, payment, refund, chargeback, loyalty, complaint, incident, accessibility, and service-recovery records
guest or traveler profile, itinerary, on-time performance, baggage, wheelchair assistance, disruption, customer-care, and travel operations records
patient or member identity, eligibility, status, service dates, consent, referral, authorization, encounter, documentation dependency, claim, remittance, denial, and utilization-review records
EHR reporting, enterprise data warehouse, BI semantic model, revenue-cycle reporting, quality reporting, scheduling analytics, and travel analytics outputs

metric dictionaries, hotel benchmark definitions, quality measure specifications, numerator and denominator logic, exclusions, value sets, HEDIS, eCQM, QRDA, benchmark, and public-reporting references
data-quality exception logs, source-reconciliation notes, refresh logs, lineage maps, disclosure-risk notes, privacy notes, PHI-handling notes, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: property management system, central reservation system, revenue management system, channel manager, and booking engine
POS, payment gateway, folio, refund, chargeback, loyalty, CRM, guest service, incident, event, and group sales systems
travel operations, itinerary, airline performance, baggage, accessibility assistance, and customer-care systems
enterprise data warehouse and governed BI semantic layer
EHR reporting and clinical documentation system when patient/member context is present
scheduling, access, referral, and authorization platforms
revenue-cycle, billing, clearinghouse, claims, remittance, denial, and payment posting systems
quality reporting, eCQM, HEDIS, registry, utilization review, and public-reporting platforms
payer, plan, eligibility, coverage, authorization, privacy, consent, disclosure, compliance, and approval workflow systems

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing hospitality, travel, healthcare, privacy, payer, or disclosure prerequisites
choose the authoritative metric definition, source hierarchy, denominator, numerator, exclusions, source tables, refresh window, confidence posture, and cadence for descriptive analytics
determine whether data quality, denominator logic, source lineage, benchmark comparability, and confidence are good enough to publish an internal analytical result
prepare metric specifications, analytical summaries, caveat notes, threshold notes, data-quality exception registers, reconciliation notes, disclosure-risk notes, and next-evidence request lists
escalate: required tenant facts, property, brand, market, room-inventory status, guest/traveler or patient/member status, payer, plan, service setting, source hierarchy, eligibility, referral, authorization, consent, accessible-room context, or retrieval hooks are missing and would change the answer
public guidance, payer policy, tenant policy, measure specifications, accessibility obligations, fee-disclosure rules, or system records materially conflict

guest privacy, payment-card sensitivity, PHI handling, privacy obligation, minimum-necessary analysis, low-volume disclosure, guest/traveler safety, patient/member safety, clinical risk, accessibility compliance, or public-reporting posture changes the answer

clinical, payer-policy, coding, medical-necessity, legal, compliance, quality attestation, revenue-management approval, refund approval, fraud review, accessibility signoff, or operational leadership authority is required
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, definitions, denominator logic, benchmark comparability, or disclosure authority is unresolved
diagnose, plan treatment, determine medical necessity, make clinical judgments, or substitute analytics for licensed review
reinterpret payer policy, coverage rules, coding policy, appeal strategy, privacy law, accessibility law, fee-disclosure law, or regulatory obligations
approve rates, refunds, service-recovery compensation, fraud determinations, accessibility compliance, safety dispositions, staffing decisions, or external disclosures
state unsupported causal, public-facing, investor-style, regulatory, benchmark, staffing, safety, pricing, refund, accessibility, or policy conclusions
disclose PHI, payment-card data, or identifiable guest/traveler/patient/member examples without minimum-necessary and approval context

domain_failure_modes: treating hospitality analytics as generic BI and failing to request property, brand, market, room-inventory, booking-channel, comp-set, payer, plan, facility, provider type, service setting, patient/member status, source hierarchy, privacy, or accessibility context

collapsing PMS, CRS, RMS, channel, folio, POS, travel, EHR, scheduling, encounter, charge, claim, remittance, denial, referral, authorization, quality, and utilization-review records into one truth set without explaining lags and ownership

publishing occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, RGI, access, quality, or utilization metrics without explicit numerator, denominator, exclusions, room-inventory treatment, measure source, cohort logic, refresh window, benchmark comparability, and public-reporting posture

inferring accessible-room compliance, fee compliance, eligibility, referral, authorization, payer coverage, medical necessity, claim status, refund authority, or denial appeal outcomes from incomplete administrative data
using PHI, payment-card data, low-volume cohorts, or identifiable guest/traveler/patient/member examples without minimum-necessary, consent, audience, and approval controls
turning descriptive trends into unsupported causal, clinical, patient-safety, guest-safety, traveler-safety, payer-policy, accessibility, compliance, pricing, refund, staffing, or legal conclusions

letting the analytics lane widen into revenue-management approval, guest-service recovery approval, licensed clinical judgment, privacy-law interpretation, coding policy, quality attestation, regulatory submission, accessibility signoff, or public disclosure approval

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed.
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting.
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data.
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome.
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific hospitality metrics, comp sets, and payer-specific operating rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Tenants differ on room inventory, out-of-order treatment, comp room handling, booking channel taxonomy, group block treatment, patient lodging rules, access, utilization, denial, and quality KPI definitions by property, market, payer, product, service setting, facility type, and contract. The package treats these as runtime prerequisites and requires public or tenant baseline plus explicit tenant override before publishing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdictional privacy, accessibility, fee-disclosure, and public-reporting requirements
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: State law, local lodging rules, accessibility requirements, payer contracts, accreditation programs, tenant policy, and payment-card obligations can be stricter than the public federal baseline. The package escalates when local privacy, accessibility, fee, disclosure, clinical-risk, or reporting authority changes the answer.

notes: Authoritative hospitality and healthcare-adjacent analytics records are the tenant-designated PMS, CRS, RMS, channel manager, POS, CRM, loyalty, travel, payment, incident, EHR, EDW, BI, scheduling, referral, authorization, claim, remittance, denial, utilization-review, quality-reporting, payer, privacy, consent, disclosure, and approval systems constrained by the cited public guidance.

The lane stops before revenue-management approval, refund approval, fraud adjudication, accessibility legal signoff, diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, payer-policy interpretation, coding-policy interpretation, privacy-law interpretation, quality attestation, public disclosure approval, regulatory submission, and patient, guest, or traveler safety judgment.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated specification-based release; unresolved items are explicit runtime prerequisites and escalation conditions.
